explanation: First generation computers (roughly 1940-1956, e.g. ENIAC, UNIVAC) were built from vacuum tubes. Transistors are the classic trap here: they define the second generation, which is why 'Both' is wrong.

An impact printer is defined by the print head physically striking an inked ribbon against the paper (dot-matrix, daisy-wheel, line printers). Laser is the trap: it is a non-impact printer, since it fuses toner without any mechanical contact.
Hardware is the term for the physical, tangible components of a computer. Software is the intangible instructions, and an operating system is one kind of software, so both are wrong.
The disk drive is the only storage device in the list. Keyboard is input, monitor and printer are output; none of them retain data.
QWERTY names the standard keyboard layout, taken from the first six letters on the top letter row.
DVD officially stands for Digital Versatile Disc. 'Digital Video Disc' is the trap: it was the early informal name, dropped once the format was used for data and audio as well as video.
The monitor displays results to the user, so it is an output device. Keyboard, joystick and microphone all feed data into the computer.

The CPU is the integrated-circuit chip that executes instructions and turns raw data into information. RAM and the hard disk only store data, and ROM only holds fixed firmware; none of them process it.

A UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply) holds a battery that supplies backup power when mains power fails, giving time to save work and shut down cleanly. It does nothing for storage capacity or speed, so 'All of the above' is wrong.
Plotter, printer and monitor are all output devices. Every other group is spoiled by one input device: scanner in A, keyboard in B, mouse in C.

A bus is the set of parallel wires on the motherboard that carries data, addresses and control signals between components. CMOS is a small memory holding BIOS settings and bays are physical slots for drives, so neither transports information.
A card cage is the metal frame or chassis with guide rails and a backplane into which circuit boards are slotted and mounted.
A printer takes data from the computer and renders it on paper for the user, which makes it an output device.
The question asks for the exception. Scanner, mouse and keyboard all send data into the computer; the printer only receives data from it, so the printer is the output device and the correct answer.
A chip card (smart card) is a credit-card-sized plastic card with an embedded microprocessor and memory. Punched tape, card punch and magnetic tape are passive legacy media with no processor.
The physical structure of a computer, the parts you can touch, is its hardware. Software is the programs, and humanware refers to the people who operate the system.
Two quick presses and releases of the left (primary) button is a double-click. A single press is primary-clicking, and a right-button press is secondary-clicking.

Notebooks, cellular phones and PDAs each have their own processor and run programs, so they are computing devices. A digital scanner is only a peripheral input device attached to a computer, so it is the exception.
